backend_policy: Python-only for repository-generated quantitative figures unless the manuscript team explicitly elects R before figure production.

figures.core_conclusion: The final analysis sample and scene design are traceable by participant group, recruitment batch, WWR, complexity, and presentation order.
figures.review_risk: Must not hide low-experience supplementation or imbalance; report before/after counts explicitly.

figures.core_conclusion: WWR and visual complexity effects on S1-S5 are evaluated as item-level outcomes rather than an unvalidated composite scale.
figures.review_risk: Avoid claiming a definitive WWR optimum from three levels; use planned-contrast language.

figures.core_conclusion: AOI-based eye metrics are interpretable only when AOI area, visit rate, and sample coverage are reported.
figures.review_risk: AOI validity must be shown before interpreting gaze allocation differences.

figures.core_conclusion: EEG interpretations are bounded by synchronization QC and convergence with eye-tracking and questionnaire evidence.
figures.review_risk: Do not present EEG-only cognitive-load claims unless multimodal convergence and alignment QC support them.

figures.core_conclusion: Gender, order/fatigue, recruitment batch, and nonlinear WWR sensitivity analyses determine how strongly each manuscript claim can be stated.
figures.review_risk: Claim strength must be downgraded when sensitivity checks or modality convergence are incomplete.
